PSYCHOLOGY|RELATIONSHIPS
An 80-year-old woman sent thousands of dollars to a man stranded in space. The story sounds absurd until you understand how romance scams actually work

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Kofi Nartey on UnsplashWhen an 80-year-old woman living alone in Japan met a man on social media, she never imagined the relationship would end with her sending thousands of dollars to an astronaut.
He told her he was an astronaut.
Over the following weeks, their conversations became deeply personal. He wasn’t just another stranger on the internet anymore. He had become someone she trusted.
Then came the emergency. He claimed his spacecraft was under attack. He was running out of oxygen. If she didn’t send money immediately, he might not survive. The woman transferred the equivalent of about $6,700.
There was just one problem. The astronaut was never in space.
He didn’t exist.
It Was Never Really About Space
At first glance, this story sounds ridiculous. An astronaut stranded in space needs money for oxygen? Most of us would dismiss that claim in seconds. But…
